reviews.comment: What the whole math department loves to do is hide behind internationals. Well, A Level Further Math & IB Advanced Math cover almost entirety of Berea math in a much more rigorous manner. Internationals are useful in many ways, like TAs, or circling them around and letting them do the explanation (so-called "groupwork", "self-discovery" etc.)
Fragile WT professor: using internationals to cover up, her ancestors did.

My high school math prof of two years (taught a special evening class) studied under Field Medalist Timothy Gowers. She scored in the top twenty in the Cambridge Tripos (a Harvard Math-55 level exam). I was one of her top students. All that I can say is this EKU bumpkin didn't understand what she's teaching.
Well, my high school math prof (taught a special class) studied under field medalist Timothy Gowers. I was one of her best students. This red-neck eku grad didn't understand what she's teaching.
There's nothing worse than an EKU grad with theorem name-dropping dissertation that doesn't know her place.
She doesn't understand what she's teaching. Not sure how she got her PhD.

DOES NOT USE A CALENDAR. Students have no idea what topics they'll learn and when, what homework they'll have, and when ANY test is scheduled. This course was 2 hours. She would lecture for 20 minutes, and spend the rest of class on her phone without engaging with the class. Students were left on their own to solve the math problem together.

She has weird ideas about math education & doesn't hesitate to shove her garbage down your throat, like other profs. The class was incredibly boring, revolving around a sneeery, poorly-written text & confused students doing explanation. I learned same topics in a far rigorous manner with a text by Putnam's top scorer & MIT PhD David Patrick.

Everything is forced. You're an object for the professors to shove whatever they think down your throat. On their side, however, expectations are nonexistent. They can't be expected to teach well, grade fairly, or do anything. The school is parasitic in that they rob four years out of students' lives for them to justify milk money out of endowment.

Know nothing. Do nothing. Blame students on everything. Exams are a mess. No feedback. Topics never taught came up. There were even "joke questions" which she didn't say were jokes. Waste your time on those. Final presentations were such a mess. Grade whatever ways she seemed fit. Lazy and always came late. All math prof same. Go to other schools.

Not accountable or responsible, just like many profs here. Never list clearly what she want and sometimes topics never taught in class appeared in exam. Always came late. Graded exams in whatever ways she wants. HWs are poorly designed and feedback is nonexistent. Don't know what others like about her. I guess their expectations are too low.

The class itself is quite interesting and somewhat challenging. However, the professor made harsh and difficult on students. The best advice is to study outside of the class. She rarely explained material which was taught, but rather did the homework in class. Get ready to spend most of your study time on this class if you want a good grade

Wonderful professor! So glad to have been placed in her class! The material was hard for me most of the time - it was Calc II but Professor Barnard was always there to lend a helping hand and is VERY flexible to work with you when you need help!
The content of the class was hard. Teaching method examples and lectures with weekly quizzes. She is helpful outside of class and curves grades/ drops low quiz scores at the end.

She was very clear and helpful. She is also one of the few professors here with a great sense of humor. Some of the quizzes were difficult but she gave the class plenty of bonus opportunities towards the end of the semester to make up any lost points. I hope I can take another class with her.

I had Barnard for multiple math courses, and I must say that she has been one of the best teachers I have ever had. She's never in a bad mood, keeps the class laughing (if you have a similar sense of humor), and somehow manages to explain the same thing 3 different ways, all in the alloted time slot. She's also extremely available outside of class.

A good teacher, will work with you if you are struggling. Class assignments are spread out well and are gone over in class. As long as you pay attention and study - and go to the math lab - you can pass this class. Lectures are long but needed to understand the material. The class is, however, orientated towards math majors so be warned.

reviews.comment: For Hitler, a lot can be blamed on the environment: great depression, defeat in WWI, hyperinflation, & a long history of antisemitism. Maybe 70% environment & 30% inborn. This one, however, is 100% inborn. Hitler did vastly more damage since he was the leader of a powerful European state. This one has to content with her little classroom pond.

She suffers from a lifelong incapacity to see any student as humans, just like her ancestors thought the whole world needs to be uplifted by WT Man's Burden (aka colonialism). The "strict" self-appointed savior who can't reverse a linked list but assumes you are a third grader, controls everything, and justifies it all in her head.
Real-life dementor from Harry Potter.

An academic Karen. Do you know why Karens call cops on others over trivia? It's due to "me to others ratio." When the value placed on self is sky-high, & others so low, a microscopic increase in your welfare justifies severe harm to others. For academic Karens, the "me" is usually their ideology, their identity group & its allies. Rest is fair-game
The closest approximation of Satan that humanity has reached so far.
I cried. I have never cried. I cried at how badly she teaches. Only she and the lord understand what she is doing, and sometimes only the lord.
Really bad
She doesn't know anything. Her goal is to gatekeep and sabotage groups she doesn't like.

If you have an attitude, you won't have fun. Not that bad, though; our class even developed good chemistry with her. Lots of meaningless writing; skip the non-technical assignments and the writing questions in labs & learn SQL or something. Quizzes are 20% of your grade, & the 2 exams are 40% of your grade. Study smart, not hard. GLHF

She does not know anything about Data Structures and doesn't teach anything in the class but rather requires students to know everything and learn from her complicated "book". She is a narcissist who wants to go up by putting other people down. She always discriminates and does not communicate professionally but bosses you around. Avoid her always
Avoid her.
She doesn't know anything about Data Structures.

Arguably, she is the worst professor in the CS department. If you can avoid taking classes with her, that will be great; she knows nothing about CS. Even if you give her easy Leetcode problems, she doesn't know how to solve them, especially if you know about CS taking any of her classes is useless unless you want to get just a credit.

She listed awards from some random college worse than Berea & made fake github commits to seem all authoritative. I am okay with profs not knowing things, but this lady was acting like Grace Hopper when she didn't even get the basics right. Of course, everyone's sexist. She put CS classes under her name so that you can't avoid her. Super annoying.

The worst professor on campus. Incompetent. She either didn't know IDE exists or didn't want you to use IDEs. Imagine doing a project on text editors! You can't seek her help both in & out of class. She tries hard to wear a fake "genius" mask. She told us she solved a hard problem in a contest but appeared she didn't even understand the problem!

Dr. Jan is the centerpiece behind the computer science major. She can bring you into the major, fresh and full of thought. She can also make you reassess stepping foot on Berea soil. All her classes are hard and you learn a lot predicated on, with enough assignments, how can you not. Look, she's genuinely nice and cares deep down. But like, wow.
She knows her business very well. Her classes are not easy but you can learn a lot if you are willing to. If you show up interest and put in the work, you will be fine.

Very biased and jittery prof. She has a few TA girls whom she tutored personally. For us, boys, we can't even ask her a question. Her typical response is "Go read the textbook!" She won't answer emails about her TAs' biased grading. Her class is full of writing. Nothing was useful. Avoid their CS curriculum.

To the person who said "She's great if you are passionate in CS", well, it's the opposite. She doesn't understand basic CS algorithms (she & those around her fool themselves that they do). Her class is full of writing reflections which will be graded on TA's whims. Basic concepts presented as rocket science.

I once went to her office hours for assistance with something in the course book I wasnt understanding. She gleefully told me she wasnt going to help and to read it again. This was a consistent pattern, showing a complete lack of interest in understanding difficulty students were facing, unless they were already doing well.

If you do your work and demonstrate a passion for computer science, you will do well in this class. However, if you do not enjoy the topic, don't bother. The course is very heavy and it mostly depends on you. There's a lot of homework outside of class, so be ready to be very diligent. Dr. Jan has a rough exterior, but she is a great professor.

This class was unnecessarily hard. It moved at a really fast pace and I don't think Dr Jan knows how to support her students well because it's very hard to ask her questions and get an answer that's helpful. The TAs were better and were helpful more so than the professor. If you can take it with a different professor please do.
She knows nothing. The class is a joke. Gave out extremely long assignments with poor instructions. Good luck trying to figure those out!

Jan's classes are writing classes in disguise, not coding classes. Well, yes, writing is important; employers like it, etc. etc; but let's leave that to writing professors--the 6 GSTR classes we are forced to take. CS classes should teach coding. So, if you wanna learn coding, please avoid. Also, Jan's grading is extremely tough--on writing!
Horrible :( Avoid at all costs. I feel like she hardly has any idea what she is talking about

Jan is probably the toughest grader at Berea. She takes off a lot of points on her tests if you fail to answer it EXACTLY the way she wants. She gives out very long, unnecessary coding homework to illustrate simple concepts. Avoid if possible.

Jan Pearce has a unique way of teaching classes. Most of the time you do an assignment such a reading a chapter about a topic and then right a report on that reading. In class she does a quiz over any material you were supposed to read. The rest of the class is then spent on team work on assignments. Learn outside class, test in class.
Jan's assignments are almost useless for teaching the course material.

She has to be one of the worst professors on campus.  She knows nothing, the class is a joke, and the material taught is kind of useless.  If you want an easy A, sure, but if you want to learn CS, go to a different college.
She is a great teacher, and I felt as though her teaching strategy was very effective with out being too hard on us. 9/10 would take another class with her.
Worst teacher I ever had. DO NOT take her.

Her Robotics class was interesting, although every lab seemed to be an experiment. Students learn practical experience from the labs, and learn the theory from the tests. Not a whole lot of out-of-class work, but a lot of sweat during the labs. She can seem quite severe in class, but she's actually quite helpful overall.
INCOMPETENT.  SHOULD NOT TEACH THIS CLASS.  LAZY. DOESN'T GRADE PAPERS.  NO FEEDBACK.  CAN'T CUT THE MUSTARD.  EMBARASSING..FELT LIKE HIGH SCHOOL. NICE BUT STICK TO MATH
She is very nice, but extremely gittery.  She teaches well, but some of her discussions seem pointless.

This professor is extremely nice, however she is kind of strange.  If you have a problem, she is willing to help but she does not reply to emails usually and she is very spastic.  It is hard relying on her for things sometimes so don't count on it always.  I have taken her classes and been her Teacher's Assistant for classes.
She's a good techer, but somewhat coo-coo.
Despite a slight tendency to treat her class like a group of third-graders, Dr. Pearce was pretty cool.  Hey-- I'm not a math person, but I survived her class.

Wonderful teacher, wonderful person. I had almost made up my mind to transfer from Berea, but after some correspondence with Jan, I have changed my mind. She has shown me that, despite my problems at Berea, this is a school where people care. Jan goes out of her way to help. I couldn't ask for a better professor.
I also had Jan for environmental sciences, she was kooky, but she always kept things interesting.  She is a good teacher, albeit a little odd in show.
She's kind of annoying.
She knows what she is talking about but doesn't show it in a clease form.  To happy about the CO2 levels in the world.  Math needs to come before other world issues because it is a MATH class.
She is a good teacher. She tries her best to make you understand the concept. Feel free to talk to her if you have any problems.
She's a great professor, she knows how to teach. Very sweet!!!
Jan is the best!!  She really cares about students.  Take her class if you have any interest in computers at all.

reviews.comment: Dr. Hines is fantastic. He's always fun, and enthusiastic about the subject he's teaching. His home-works aren't that long, and he is really generous with test notes. If you pay attention and do the work class with him will be a breeze.

GREAT Professor, supportive and cares about the success of his students. There were 3 normal exams, and your lowest exam score could substitute your final exam score if you didn't take it. Your final exam score could also replace your lowest exam score. The only thing I noticed was that some of the homework problems he gave us were not covered.

Firstly, he uses free textbooks!! Secondly, he has no real late-work penalty, he also assigns homework a week ahead so you have chances to ask any questions you may have. I genuinely loathe math, but he has made it bearable.

I took Dr. Gratton for MAT 104 during the summer. The format of the class was really easy and clear. You get time during class to do the homework, while he goes around asking about problems or doubts. He makes you think instead of giving the answer, but that makes you actually learn.
I thought Dr. Gratton was a great prof. He was clear, engaging, flexible, and accommodating. I was really grateful that I took MAT 104 with him. If you need to take this class, take it with him.

Amazing professor! Truly cares about his students

reviews.comment: DOES NOT USE A CALENDAR. Has a half-page syllabus with zero information of topics to learn. Students have no idea what topics they'll learn and when, what homework they'll have, and when ANY test is scheduled. Speaks fast. Jumps right into lecture without background information. Office hours are not enough to help.

-Gives partial credit on exams and homework
-Homework every night, 4-6 exams, no quizzes 
-Class participation is not required, but is helpful for learning the material 
-Helpful during office hours and willing to meet on zoom outside of his set hours

A good math prof, but his tests are loaded with "Assume this is true (it's not)" so he checks your understanding. If you don't understand the content, you won't get a good grade. I thought his TA graded way too hard but he gives a good amount of points on the test. He's also a really chill guy. BEWARE THE GATEWAY EXAMS!!! TAKE THEM EARLY!!!!!

He doesnt go over the topics of the homework before he assigns it. Its really a learn for yourself process. If this is your style of learning you prefer then his class is perfect for you but if you prefer to go over what the homework is gonna be about then this isnt the teacher for you. He is a super chill and casual guy though.
Students love him.
this professor is awesome!
He's a genius!
He's simply the best. Well, atleast for Cal. II. I enjoyed the class. He stands in the top 2 of about 9 Math teachers I have had in my life so far.

James is the best teacher ever, I expected CAL III to be really boring but he just makes it so interesting. I will definitely take him again and again. He is so funny and easygoing and helpful, even when you ask for help not related to the class at all.

James is a good teacher, he keeps his students awake and involved without pressuring them in class. He is very by the book grade-wise and will hold to his syllabus guidelines. If you want to learn the math then you won't have any problems! (Oh and turn in the homeworks, they're worth more than it seems)

He is very scattered and full of energy at the same time. As a result, lectures are delivered fast while he makes thousands of different moves in front of the blackboard. He is so casual that I just cannot take the classes seriously. Very hard grader. His tests are awful because he would ask for counterexample or something egregiously obvious.

He discriminates against international and non-math/science students,especially economics students. At least, he acted that way in our class. 20 people signed up and at the end, there were only 9 people left. He is smart and he likes his job. But he is sometimes not clear and simple to understand.

reviews.comment: The goal of the professor is more to ascertain how smart she is & dispel her insecurities than to teach students. She often told us it took her 15 mins to do the homework. Excuse me? Didn't you do a Ph.D. in math? Made everything stressful, chaotic, & unpredictable. It seems to be a feature of the white mind to feel pleasure in others' suffering.
She left the college and academia forever. Guys, time to stop ranting about her.

What I would like to add is that her assignments aren't useful. The feedback is nonexistent and the class looked like torture where little real learning happens. Depending on how you perceive her class, she can be very rude at you and leash out. A few friends were kicked out. So be careful if you have to take the class.
I honestly dont know what people dislike about Professor Kelly! She is kind and has done a good job teaching stats! I would definitely take this class again!
Awful teacher who is so rude to students and inconsiderate. She makes everything so complicated and gets pissed off easily. Not a good teacher at all.

Prof. Kelly, although very nice, often comes into class obviously unprepared. Get ready to teach yourself (Youtube!!). Her TA is great, but she relies too much on group exercises and will make you do the work before explaining everything you need to know. She gets confused when you don't get it and to me, made stats more complicated than it is.

Scary. Subjective. Chaotic. She asked a friend never to come the class because he wasn't taking notes, even though he was paying full attention. Occasionally, you will be graded for criteria not explicitly mentioned by her. For example, she would give assignments without asking you to explain, but she would give you zero for "lacking explanations."
Wouldnt lecture in class. Expected us to watch videos of her outside of class to learn material. Spent most of my time on YouTube or Khan Academy to help with the actual homework or study for tests.

I had to take this class for my CS degree and it added nothing to it. She could make the simplest thing sound like rocket science. She grades you for things she doesnt ask you to do. Ive lost many points because I was supposed to explain my answer, even though that wasnt stated in the question. Shes also be kind of rude. Dont take her classes.

Operations research is a tough class, but Dr. Kelly made it unbearably difficult and boring. She gives unclear instructions in her lectures and her assignment instructions. In short, she simply does not explain and present her material well. She also often makes mistakes in her teaching, which makes the material more confusing. Her grading is harsh

I believe she cares about her students, but I'm not clear whether it is a veneer. The more I take her classes, the more I hate math. She is not clear about what she wants. The way she explains things is non-intuitive, and you'll need a lot of memorization and tedious homework. She makes math boring and probably has some ego issues.

I hated her class. She gives writing assignments which she grades harder than a writing class (she gave me a 0 on one). She wants you to write detailed explanations on her homework even if it is a repetitive question with different numbers and grades them harshly. She lectures for long times which makes me want to sleep every time.  Don't take her.

Prof. Kelly is awful. She isn't clear and forces you to participate in unnecessary group work. She also doesn't teach the material effectively and then expects you to learn it from your group members (who also have no clue what is going on). Simply stated, I go through crossings, hoping to get hit by a vehicle, so I don't have to attend this class.

reviews.comment: Dr. Stinebrickner is the best math professor I had in college. The analytical skills that I learned from him, I still use today.  His teaching inspired me to become better and passionate about math. He is not the kind of professor that will assume you know what he knows which most professors just tend to do.

Dr. Ralph gets very excited by math - he has a lot of enthusiasm and that is evident in class. He's willing to help out and honestly tries to get students to understand the material. The only problem that I had was that he would assign homework from the book which consists of mostly numerical problems,while his tests are mostly word problems.

Dr. Stinebrickner really has a passion for what he does. The best part of his class is his enthusiasm. You won't have to hesitate to ask to go over problems because sometimes the entire class block goes by during his review of the last homework assignment. He goes to great lengths to give as many examples as possible and grades fairly.
